channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Zohran Mamdani's Actions and Achievements in His First Four Months
item.description: In just four months, Zohran Mamdani, a recently elected political figure, has made a noticeable impact on his community. Rather than pursuing grandiose projects or political theatrics, Mamdani has foc...

item.title: The 1902 Baker Electric Runabout: A Pioneering Electric Vehicle
item.description: The 1902 Baker Electric Runabout is an early example of electric vehicle innovation, manufactured by the Baker Motor Vehicle Company in Cleveland, Ohio. Unlike the noisy and cumbersome gasoline cars o...

item.title: McArthur Wheeler's Infamous Bank Robbery and the Birth of the Dunning–Kruger Effect
item.description: In 1995, McArthur Wheeler, a 44-year-old man from Pittsburgh, carried out a bizarre bank robbery that would later become famous for its psychological implications. Believing that lemon juice could ren...

item.title: Audit recommends Inuit-led reforms for Nunavik Police Service to improve representation and community safety
item.description: A recent audit of the Nunavik Police Service (NPS), commissioned by the Kativik Regional Government (KRG) and Makivvik, recommends a range of reforms to establish a “distinctly Inuit public safety sys...

item.title: Court extends Anmol alias Pinky's physical remand in murder case amid torture allegations
item.description: In Karachi, alleged drug queenpin Anmol, also known as Pinky, has been remanded into police custody for four more days in connection with a murder case. She faces at least 15 ongoing criminal cases, i...

item.title: Experts Explain Why Seasonal Allergies Are Intensifying Across Canada
item.description: Seasonal allergies are becoming more severe in Canada, according to health experts. Increased pollen levels, changing weather patterns, and longer warm seasons are contributing to more intense allergi...

item.title: Rotterdam authorities quarantine crew and disinfect cruise ship after hantavirus outbreak
item.description: The MV Hondius, a luxury polar expedition cruise ship, has docked in Rotterdam after a hantavirus outbreak affected passengers and crew. Dutch authorities are quarantining the remaining 27 people on b...

item.title: Amazon Launches AI-Powered Podcasts Through Alexa+ With Customizable Topics
item.description: Amazon has introduced a new feature for its Alexa+ AI voice assistant that generates podcasts entirely using artificial intelligence. The company announced that Alexa+ can now produce full audio episo...

item.title: Bernadette Peters’ Early Breakthrough in Dames at Sea
item.description: Bernadette Peters first gained widespread attention for her performance as Ruby in the 1966 Off-Off-Broadway production of “Dames at Sea” at Caffe Cino. She reprised the role in the 1968 Off-Broadway ...

item.title: Kenyan Women Admire Aga Khan Cardiologist After TV Interview
item.description: A section of Kenyan women have expressed admiration for Dr Mohamed Jeilani, a cardiologist at Aga Khan University Hospital, following his recent appearance on live TV. During the broadcast, Dr Jeilani...

item.title: Twisha Sharma's Father Seeks Fresh Autopsy; UP CM Warns on Namaz on Roads; Key National Developments
item.description: In today’s top news, the father of Twisha Sharma, who was found dead at her in-laws' house in Bhopal, has requested a fresh post-mortem at AIIMS-Delhi, alleging a potential cover-up. Navnidhi Sharma r...

item.title: WWII Seaplane Lands Safely on Phoenix Street Following Engine Failure
item.description: A Second World War-era seaplane had to perform an emergency landing on a street in Phoenix, Arizona, on 12 April after experiencing engine trouble mid-flight. Footage captured by a camera mounted on t...

item.title: Planned London Underground strikes called off following negotiations
item.description: Planned strike action by London Underground drivers, initially scheduled for 19 and 21 May, has been called off following last-minute discussions between the Rail, Maritime and Transport (RMT) union a...

item.title: Amaechi Says Economic Hardship Affects Both Nigerians and Governors Under Tinubu
item.description: Former Rivers State Governor, Rotimi Amaechi, don talk say Nigerians no dey alone suffer under President Bola Tinubu administration, even governors dey complain. Amaechi yarn this one for DCL Hausa pr...

item.title: Saad Edhi and international activists detained by Israeli forces while delivering humanitarian aid to Gaza
item.description: On Monday, Saad Edhi, son of renowned Pakistani humanitarian Faisal Edhi, along with several other members of the Global Sumud Flotilla, were reportedly detained by Israeli forces in the eastern Medit...

item.title: Examining the Rise of Christianity and Nationalist Influences in the UK
item.description: In recent decades, Britain appeared to be moving away from Christianity, with declining church attendance and a more secular society. However, a recent report has suggested that church attendance may ...

item.title: Debate Over Starlink’s Entry into South Africa Amid BEE and Investment Rules

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard confronts Chinese vessel near Pag-asa Island in West Philippine Sea
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) has recently challenged a Chinese research vessel operating near Pag-asa Island in the West Philippine Sea. This incident reflects the ongoing maritime tensions in the...

item.title: Harry Styles brings unity and joy to Amsterdam in intimate world tour performance
